mnemonics: DDL DML DCL: Data Definition Language (DDL) creates the house structure, Data Manipulation Language (DML) is for living in it (moving furniture/people), and Data Control Language (DCL) is for the keys and security system.
keyPoints: DDL (Data Definition Language) is used to define database schemas (e.g., CREATE, ALTER, DROP).
DML (Data Manipulation Language) is used for managing data within schema objects (e.g., SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE).
DCL (Data Control Language) is used to control access to data (e.g., GRANT, REVOKE).
commonMistakes: Confusing DDL and DML commands (e.g., using DROP, a DDL command, to try and delete table rows instead of DELETE, a DML command).
Forgetting that DCL is separate from DML and is specifically for granting and revoking user permissions, not for querying or updating data.
